object_under_evaluation: integrity of memo recall on explicit inspect/capsule/expand consumer paths
claim_boundary: On this bounded memo-guardrail family, the consumer can often keep recall compact and provenance-visible, but that support weakens when lifecycle posture blurs or when memo grounding is too thin for the consumer path being attempted.
limitations: This report does not prove contradiction handling quality.
This report does not prove permission or authority safety from memo fields.
This report does not prove general memory quality.
strongest_recall_signal: Inspect stayed smaller than full expansion on the precision case while still keeping the provenance-thread boundary visible.
strongest_recall_risk: A stale memo surface remained compact but too easy to read as current until the consumer escalated to a stronger source.

case_notes.provenance_note: The compact recall kept the provenance-thread pointer visible instead of treating the capsule as self-justifying proof.
case_notes.staleness_note: Lifecycle posture remained current and did not require expansion to see that the claim was still active.
case_notes.stronger_source_note: No stronger-source escalation was needed because the compact surface stayed bounded and sufficient.

case_notes.provenance_note: The provenance pointer survived, but the consumer had to lift to a stronger source because memo grounding alone was too thin.
case_notes.staleness_note: The recalled claim showed superseded posture only after the stronger source was checked against the lifecycle surface.
case_notes.stronger_source_note: Escalation was the honest move, but the compact memo surface looked cleaner than the lifecycle evidence justified.
